_comment: Microsoft Defender for IoT — Layer 2 Network Detection Policy Template
_usage: Import via Defender for IoT portal: Settings > Detection > Custom Alerts > Import Policy. Replace all placeholder values before import.
description: Layer 2 network detection policy for ARP integrity, unauthorized device, and VLAN hopping detection. Maps to NIST SC-7, AC-3, AC-4.

alert_destinations._comment: Replace workspace_id and shared_key with your Log Analytics Workspace values from Azure Portal > Log Analytics Workspace > Agents Management
Replace with your syslog/SIEM host. Use TCP (port 6514) for production to ensure delivery.

detection_rules.description: Detects ARP spoofing (T1557.002 — LLMNR/NBT-NS Poisoning and SMB Relay). Triggered when a MAC address claims an IP address already associated with a different MAC in the device inventory. Indicates man-in-the-middle attack setup at Layer 2.
detection_rules.nist_description: SC-7: Boundary Protection — Layer 2 boundary integrity violated by ARP cache poisoning

conditions._comment: Fires when ARP reply asserts a MAC-IP pair that conflicts with the known inventory

threshold._comment: Alert on first occurrence — ARP spoofing is always high confidence

detection_rules.investigation_notes: Check arpwatch flip-flop log. Identify both MACs. Locate physical port of unexpected MAC. Isolate if confirmed malicious. See playbooks/04-triage-alerts.md.

detection_rules.description: Detects a new device connecting to the network that is not in the approved device inventory (T1200 — Hardware Additions). New MAC addresses on the network that were not pre-approved may represent rogue devices, unauthorized workstations, or IoT devices inserted by an insider threat.
detection_rules.nist_description: AC-3: Access Enforcement — only authorized devices should have Layer 2 network access

conditions._comment: Fires when a MAC is seen for the first time and is not in the allowlist
Replace with monitored subnet CIDR e.g. 10.10.0.0/16

threshold._comment: Alert after device is seen 3 times in 5 minutes to reduce false positives from DHCP renewal

detection_rules.investigation_notes: Look up MAC OUI to identify vendor. Cross-reference with asset management (CMDB). If not authorized, trace to switch port and disable port. Escalate if appears to be deliberate insertion.

detection_rules.description: Detects VLAN hopping attacks (T1599 — Network Boundary Bridging) via double-tagging or DTP exploitation. An attacker on VLAN 10 can send double-tagged frames to reach VLAN 20 if the native VLAN is VLAN 1 and port security is misconfigured. This represents a critical AC-4 information flow violation.
detection_rules.nist_description: AC-4: Information Flow Enforcement — VLAN boundaries must prevent unauthorized cross-VLAN traffic

conditions._comment: Double-tagged frames (802.1q in 802.1q) are the primary indicator of VLAN hopping

additional_indicators._comment: Outer tag of 1 (native VLAN) with inner tag targeting a different VLAN is classic double-tag hopping
DTP frames from an access port indicate an attempt to negotiate trunk mode

threshold._comment: Any double-tagged frame is an immediate critical alert — not expected in normal operations

detection_rules.investigation_notes: Immediately identify source MAC and port. Capture frame samples. Disable source port. If DTP is involved, verify all access ports have 'switchport nonegotiate' configured. Review native VLAN settings per 02a-fix-AC3-vlan-segmentation.md.

baseline_settings._comment: During learning period, new devices are logged but not alerted. After learning period ends, L2-002 fires on unknown MACs.
approved_mac_prefixes: _comment: Add known-good OUI prefixes (first 3 octets) for your hardware vendors e.g. 00:50:56 for VMware
